text.title: Cohort Based Mini Course Launch Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Cohort Based Mini Course Launch application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Cohort MUST be ≤20 seats. Larger = operator cannot grade individual work; smaller = price point unsustainable.
rule.rationale: Cohort-based-course practice — anchors Cohort Based Mini Course Launch rule `cohort-cap-twenty`.

rule.statement: Mini-course price MUST sit in $99-$499. Below = perceived no-value; above = audience expects a full course product.
rule.rationale: Indie pricing research — anchors Cohort Based Mini Course Launch rule `price-band-99-499`.

rule.statement: Launch playbook MUST complete in ≤6 weeks (waitlist → graduation). Longer = operator runs out of energy and audience attention fades.
rule.rationale: Launch-cadence research — anchors Cohort Based Mini Course Launch rule `six-week-cap`.

rule.statement: ≥4 live sessions MUST be included (recorded for replay). Pure async = ebook value, not cohort value.
rule.rationale: Cohort-based-course design — anchors Cohort Based Mini Course Launch rule `live-session-required`.

rule.statement: By session 5, the post-graduation upsell offer MUST be presented (next-cohort, 1:1, or product). No upsell = audience disperses post-graduation.
rule.rationale: Funnel research — anchors Cohort Based Mini Course Launch rule `upsell-hook-pre-graduation`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
